name: Calaceite, La Fresneda y Ermita de Santa Ana y Los Santos — circular por la comarca de Matarraña
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Calaceite traverse the Matarraña region, characterized by rolling hills, olive groves, and terraced vineyards. The landscape features river valleys, such as those formed by the Matarraña and Algás rivers, and extends towards the rugged terrain of Els Ports Natural Park. This area offers diverse natural features, including viewpoints and a mix of open country and more sheltered paths. Best mountain bike trails around Calaceite The most popular mountain bike trail is Calaceite, La Fresneda y Ermita de Santa Ana y Los Santos — circular por la comarca de Matarraña, a 40.9 miles (66 km) trail that takes 5 hours 32 minutes to complete. This difficult circular route explores the wider Matarraña region, connecting several towns and historical sites. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Calaceite – Calaceite Old Town loop from Calaceite / Calaceit, a moderate 23.6 miles (38 km) path. This loop offers views of Calaceite's historic architecture and surrounding rural landscapes. Local mountain bikers also love the Valderrobres Stone Bridge – Cretas Historic Town Centre loop from Calaceite / Calaceit, a 31.4 miles (51 km) trail leading through the scenic countryside between Valderrobres and Cretas, often completed in about 4 hours 3 minutes. Mountain biking around Calaceite is defined by olive groves, vineyards, and river valleys, with routes connecting traditional towns and offering panoramic viewpoints. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging long-distance routes. The routes in Calaceite are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 30 reviews. More than 500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Calaceite's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Calaceite?
A: There are over 40 mountain bike trails around Calaceite listed on komoot, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. The region is known for its diverse landscapes, from rolling hills to river valleys.

Q: What kind of terrain and difficulty levels can I expect on mountain bike trails near Calaceite?
A: The trails around Calaceite traverse varied terrain, including olive groves, vineyards, and river valleys, extending towards the rugged landscapes of Els Ports Natural Park. You'll find options ranging from easy, gentle paths to challenging routes with significant elevation gain. Specifically, there are 2 easy, 35 moderate, and 4 difficult trails listed on komoot.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in the Calaceite area?
A: Yes, the region offers family-friendly options. The "Via Verde del Valle de Zafán" is an old railway route converted into a greenway, providing an accessible and scenic option suitable for families. Sections range from 10 to 41 kilometers, winding through forests, ravines, tunnels, and bridges, particularly near the Natural Park of the Ports de Beceit.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or natural features I can see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails often pass through picturesque landscapes, including the Matarraña and Algás river valleys, traditional towns, and olive groves. You can also explore natural attractions like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4961316" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Els Estrets Gorge&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4959065" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Toll Blau&lt;/a&gt;. The area also boasts historical sites such as the Iberian archaeological site of San Antonio and 18th-century buildings in Calaceite's Old Town.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, many routes around Calaceite are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20143779" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calaceite – Calaceite Old Town loop from Calaceite / Calaceit&lt;/a&gt;, which offers views of the town's historic architecture and rural surroundings.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Calaceite?
A: The Matarraña region generally offers pleasant conditions for mountain biking for much of the year. Spring and autumn are particularly ideal, with milder temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended, while winters are generally mild but can have cooler days.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Calaceite?
A: The mountain bike trails around Calaceite are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 30 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse landscapes, the mix of challenging and accessible routes, and the charming traditional towns that dot the region, often referred to as the 'Tuscany of Spain'.

Q: Are there any specific facilities for mountain bikers in the region?
A: Yes, the region is becoming increasingly bike-friendly. The Matarraña Bike Park in Fuentespalda offers five downhill trails and an uplift service. Additionally, some local establishments, like Hotel Posada Guadalupe in Monroyo, provide services such as bike repair stations, washing areas, secure storage, e-bike charging points, and route information.

Q: Can I find mountain bike routes that connect to other towns in the Matarraña region?
A: Absolutely. Many routes are designed to connect charming villages and towns. For instance, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40012847" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valderrobres Stone Bridge – Cretas Historic Town Centre loop from Calaceite / Calaceit&lt;/a&gt; takes you through the scenic countryside between Valderrobres and Cretas, allowing you to explore more of the Matarraña's unique character.

Q: Are there any trails that offer panoramic viewpoints?
A: Yes, the mountainous terrain surrounding Calaceite provides numerous opportunities for magnificent viewpoints. Many routes are designed to take advantage of the elevated positions, offering expansive vistas over the olive groves, vineyards, and traditional towns of the Matarraña region.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the mountain bike trails?
A: While many trails in natural areas are generally accessible to dogs, it's always best to check specific local regulations or signage for each route. When riding with your dog, ensure they are well-behaved, kept under control, and that you clean up after them to respect other trail users and wildlife.

Q: Are there any long-distance or multi-day mountain biking options?
A: For more ambitious cyclists, the Pedales del Matarraña offers a challenging 194 km BTT route with 4,900 meters of elevation, designed to be completed over several days. This allows for a deeper immersion into the region's diverse landscapes and charming villages.
tips: Beautiful typical Spanish town with that striking tower. Note that this town is also on a top, so climb up quite a bit before you get there and once you're there you'll quickly be on the via Verde. Various MTB routes are well signposted around the town. The routes can be quite long.
At this point on the Vía Verde del Val de Zafán we find a beautiful picture next to the bridge near the town of Cretas.

We are in Calaceite, a Spanish municipality in the province of Teruel located in the Matarraña region. We highlight to visit the Iberian towns such as San Antonio, the beautiful Plaza Mayor and the parish church.

waypoints.name: Stone Arch Bridge on the Vía Verde del Val de Zafán near Cretas
